schema_notes: Per-billionaire record matching ellison.v3 schema. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment). Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream.

person.wealth_source: Dell Technologies founder/CEO (~45-50% stake); also Broadcom stake (from 2023 VMware sale to Broadcom for $68B); DFO Management / MSD Capital family office; Secureworks stake

net_worth.range_note: Forbes 2026 lists him at #13; Bloomberg 2025 ~$151B (10th richest); Inside Philanthropy Nov 2025 cited ~$112B post-net-worth-doubling. Bajaj tracker ~$135B. Dell's stake is heavily concentrated in Dell Technologies (liquid equity but large block) plus private DFO holdings. Mid-point ~$135B used as best estimate.

sources.value_note: Forbes 2026 profile — exact figure behind bot-wall; Forbes index lists rank #13. Bajaj tracker and Bloomberg cross-cites ~$135B range.
Nov 2025 IP piece cited net worth 'north of $112B' after ~2x growth over 2 years.
April 2026 piece notes Dell 'back in top 10' after giving away $6.25B.

net_worth.liquidity_notes: ~50% stake in Dell Technologies (public, but concentrated block — divestment would move stock); Broadcom stake (public, more liquid as minority); DFO/MSD Capital family office holdings are private. Apply 35% liquidity discount for 'giving capacity now' calculations — higher than Ellison's 25% because Dell's positions are somewhat more diversified post-Broadcom.

cited_events.recipient: Various (ProPublica summary; recipients include charter schools, education, health)
cited_events.notes: Charitable disbursements per 990-PF. Inside Philanthropy reports 2024 grantmaking of ~$308M (different definition possibly including DAF sub-granting); reconcile before publish.

cited_events.dedup_note: This is a transfer INTO MSDF ($140M) and into DAFs ($210M). Per dedup rule #2, we count grants OUT of the foundation (which we have via 990-PF) and grants OUT of DAFs (which are donor-opaque and not visible). The $140M portion is already captured in MSDF's subsequent grant_out flows; the $210M DAF portion is observable only as a transfer, not as final giving. Mark as transfer_in and EXCLUDE from observable rollup to avoid double-counting with MSDF grants; flag $210M DAF portion as potential hidden giving (destination unknown).

CRITICAL: $5B grew MSDF endowment from ~$1.5B (2022) to $7.5B+ (2024). This is transfer INTO foundation, not out-the-door giving. Observable giving is the foundation's grants_out, not the endowment injection. Under Forbes/IPS methodology, endowment transfers are NOT counted as philanthropy until distributed to charity. Excluded from observable rollup.

cited_events.notes: Michael Dell has NOT signed the Giving Pledge. Multiple sources confirm: Vox (2019) lists Dell as a conspicuous tech-billionaire absentee alongside Page/Brin/Ballmer; Inside Philanthropy (Nov 2025) explicitly states 'the Dells themselves' have not signed; IBTimes lists him among richest non-signers. The Dells' philanthropy proceeds without a formal public lifetime-giving commitment.

Susan Dell stated in 2025: the Dells have 'given away over $3 billion over the past 26 years, mostly to children's causes.' This self-report is a rough upper bound claim. Our MSDF 990-PF sum 2019-2024 alone = $1.064B; pre-2019 MSDF 990-PF data not yet pulled. The $3B self-report is roughly consistent with cumulative MSDF grants_out 1999-2024 given lower annual grantmaking in the 2000s-2010s.

pledges_and_announcements.recipient: Invest America / 'Trump Accounts' — $250 per account to ~25M children in ZIP codes with median income <$150k, for children age 10 or under. Accounts invest in US index funds; distributions taxed on withdrawal.
pledges_and_announcements.classification_note: AMBIGUOUS_CLASSIFICATION: Trump Accounts are private investment accounts in the child's name (not a 501(c)(3) recipient). Forbes (Erb, Dec 2025) analyzed the tax treatment: the contribution is likely structured as gifts to individual minor accounts, NOT a charitable deduction to a 501(c)(3). Under strict Forbes/IPS/Chronicle methodology, transfers into individual investment accounts do NOT count as charitable giving. However, the recipient-selection logic (low-income ZIP codes, children) has a charitable flavor. Conservative treatment: EXCLUDE from observable charitable giving pending clarification of IRS/legal structure. Flag for reclassification when disbursement mechanism is clarified (could route through a 501(c)(3) 'Invest America Foundation' or similar, in which case it would count).

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1.
political_giving.observed_total_note: No major super-PAC giving identified in press review. CIO.com (older) cites modest $2,500-$5,000 donations to Texas Republicans (McCaul, Lamar Smith, Carter) plus Dell corporate PAC. OpenSecrets donor lookup returned 226 records but direct scrape failed — deferred to Phase 1.3 FEC integration. Dell's political profile appears moderate-to-low relative to his net worth, consistent with his public positioning as bipartisan/technocratic. Dell serves on Trump's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology (2026 reappointment) — access signals political engagement but not large-dollar donation pattern.

sources.amount_note: Modest individual contributions to Texas Republicans

foundations_active.name: Michael & Susan Dell Foundation
foundations_active.assets_usd_growth_note: Assets grew ~5x from 2022 ($1.52B) to 2024 ($7.77B) following $5B in personal contributions from the Dells in Dec 2023 ($3.6B cash+stock) and 2024 ($1.4B). Per Inside Philanthropy, this was timed with Dell's post-Broadcom VMware-sale liquidity event.
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants_out ($218.9M) / average assets (~$6.4B between 2023 $5.26B and 2024 $7.53B year-end) ≈ 3.4%. BELOW the 5% legal minimum for a single year. HOWEVER: IRS 5% rule is on a rolling/distributable-amount basis, and a foundation that just received a large contribution has a 12-month grace window plus can count 'qualifying distributions' toward future obligations. Inside Philanthropy flags: the Dells added $5B to endowment but grantmaking only rose to $308M (their number, which likely includes DAF sub-granting) — in absolute growth terms, the foundation is accumulating wealth faster than it is deploying it. Flag LOW_PAYOUT_RATIO as watch item; NOT a hard legal violation given grace period.
foundations_active.compensation_flag: NORMAL. Michael and Susan Dell receive zero compensation. Staff compensation for a $7.5B foundation with global operations (US, India, Africa, Israel) is within industry norms for grantmaking foundations of this size.
foundations_active.red_flag_daf_transfers: CONFIRMED. Oct 2023 Michael Dell personal stock gift of $350M split $140M to MSDF + $210M to UNSPECIFIED DAFs (per PND, Forbes, Philanthropy News Digest). This is a DAF red flag because DAF disbursements are donor-opaque — we cannot verify whether the $210M was onward-granted to 501(c)(3)s or warehoused indefinitely. The DAFs themselves are not named; MSDF 990-PF grant recipients list should be checked for DAF beneficiary transfers as additional signal.

dafs_detected.name: Unspecified DAF(s) receiving Oct 2023 stock gift
dafs_detected.note: PND and Forbes both report $210M portion went to 'several donor-advised funds.' Specific DAF sponsors not named in public reporting. This is classic DAF opacity: we know money went in, cannot verify what went out.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: At least one confirmed DAF transfer ($210M in 2023). Specific DAF sponsors not named. Subsequent disbursements from these DAFs are not publicly observable. Apply hidden_upper uplift conservatively; warehouse risk non-zero.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: DFO Management / MSD Capital is Michael Dell's family office / personal investment vehicle — NOT a philanthropic LLC like CZI or Emerson Collective. No philanthropic LLC structure identified.
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2. Dell's DFO family office is Delaware/Austin-based per public disclosures; no prominent offshore structure identified in press.
detected_vehicles.trusts_note: No public disclosure of a charitable trust structure analogous to Ellison's. Dell Technologies proxy filings reference standard family trusts for stock holdings but no charitable remainder/lead trust has been publicly disclosed.

red_flags.evidence: Oct 2023 stock gift: $210M of $350M total routed to unspecified donor-advised funds (60% of gift). DAF disbursements are donor-opaque — no downstream verification possible.

MSDF 2024 grants_out ($218.9M) vs assets ($7.77B) = ~2.8%. Foundation received $5B injection in 2023-2024 but grantmaking only rose to $218.9M (990-PF) or $308M (IP reported). IRS 5% rule has grace periods post-contribution but this pattern — asset accumulation faster than deployment — is the 'warehousing-via-foundation' pattern IPS critiques.

$6.25B Dec 2025 pledge to Trump Accounts is announced as philanthropy but funds individual minor investment accounts (not a 501(c)(3) recipient). Tax-deductible status unclear per Forbes tax analysis. Under strict charitable giving methodology this transfer does NOT qualify as philanthropy; it is closer to private wealth transfer to low-income children.

Multiple sources (Vox 2019, IP Nov 2025, IBTimes) confirm Dell has not signed the Giving Pledge despite 34+ years of billionaire status and ~$135B net worth. This is an absence-of-formal-commitment flag, not a breach flag. Softens once lifetime giving is large (Dell's ~$3B+ is material but ~2% of net worth).

red_flags_not_present: HIGH_OFFICER_COMP (Michael and Susan Dell take zero comp; staff comp is normal for foundation size)
POLITICAL_DOMINATES_CHARITABLE (modest individual political giving, not super-PAC-scale like Ellison)

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(MSDF 990-PF grants_out 2019-2024). Excludes: $5B endowment injection 2023-2024 (transfer_in); $350M Oct 2023 stock gift (transfer_in, $140M portion to MSDF is captured in 2024 MSDF grants_out as it disburses, $210M DAF portion is donor-opaque); $6.25B Trump Accounts pledge (ambiguous classification, not yet disbursed, likely not 501(c)(3)); $750M UT Austin Apr 2026 pledge (not yet disbursed); pre-2019 MSDF grants (not yet pulled).
observable_giving_todo: Pull MSDF 990-PF years 1999-2018 to add to observable; likely adds ~$1.5-2B based on foundation age and grant cadence.
Verify $140M Oct 2023 MSDF-bound stock gift is captured in MSDF 2024 grants_out (not double-count).
Verify $210M Oct 2023 DAF recipient(s) — if named in SEC Form 4 footnotes or subsequent disclosures, track downstream.
Reconcile MSDF $218.9M (990-PF) vs $308M (IP 2024 reported grantmaking) — may differ by timing of stock gift in-kind valuation.

Track $6.25B Trump Accounts disbursement structure: if routed through a 501(c)(3) Invest America foundation, reclassify as direct_gift observable giving; if routed as gifts to individual accounts, keep EXCLUDED.
Run SEC EDGAR Form 4 for Michael Dell CIK 1383812 for pre-2023 and post-2023 G-transactions.
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.35 × $135B = $47.25B; tenure cap saturated (34 years, well over 10). Expected = $4.73B.

hidden_upper_usd.daf_uplift_note: Confirmed $210M transferred to unnamed DAFs in Oct 2023. Under standard DAF-opacity treatment, this full amount goes to hidden upper bound (we cannot verify disbursement). May be double-counted as transfer_in — net treatment: include full $210M as hidden-giving upper bound.
hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked (Phase 2). Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of older HNW donors give anonymously at least sometimes. Dell's public posture (per IP: 'not the billionaire couple on the front page' — quiet philanthropy, Susan chairs foundation) skews toward anonymous giving. Apply 0.2% of liquid wealth as upper: 0.002 × $135B × 0.35 liquidity = $95M; but amplify ~5x given IP's explicit characterization of quiet posture = $500M.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: No publicly disclosed religious affiliation with tithing norms. Zero.
hidden_upper_usd.pre_2019_msdf_method: MSDF founded 1999; grants 1999-2018 not yet pulled. Extrapolate from 1999-2018 average assets (~$1-1.5B) × ~5-10% grantmaking rate × 20 years = ~$1.5-2B. Middle estimate $1.8B. REVISE when MSDF 1999-2018 990-PF data retrieved.

rollup.tier_rule: Tier A (Verified Low) requires observable + hidden < 0.2 × expected — Dell fails this (observable alone is 0.225). Tier B (Probably Low) requires 0.2-0.5 — Dell is in this range ONLY if we don't backfill MSDF 1999-2018 and don't credit DAF transfers. With reasonable backfill (observable + hidden ≈ $3.57B), Dell's ratio is ~0.76 of expected, landing in Tier C 'On Track' or 'Moderate Giver.' CRITICAL factor: MSDF is a 25-year-old serious working foundation with zero-comp principals, global operations, and a $5B 2023-2024 endowment injection signaling future scale-up. Dell is a HIGH-GIVING case relative to Tier A billionaires.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Readers should see Dell as the anti-Ellison: did NOT sign Giving Pledge but HAS built a serious working foundation over 25 years, zero-comp principals, broad operations. Key tensions: (1) $5B endowment injection accumulating faster than deploying (low payout ratio watch); (2) $210M DAF transfer opacity; (3) $6.25B Trump Accounts pledge is NOT conventional charity. Dell is notable for doing real philanthropy without the public commitment apparatus (Pledge, LLC, splashy vehicles).
rollup.expected_5pct_tenure_method: 5% × net_worth_best × liquidity_pct × years_as_billionaire (uncapped tenure); computed by aggregate_v3.py
rollup.observable_reconciliation_note: Published `observable_giving_usd` reflects agent judgment about attributable giving. `observable_from_events_usd` is the strict sum of canonical grant_out + direct_gift event amounts. A gap can mean self-reported lifetime figures beyond cited events (rollup > sum) or exclusion of inherited-foundation / family-foundation portions (rollup < sum).

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Computed with v2 formula for continuity. Giving gap is small (observable/expected = 0.225; gap = 1 - 0.225 = 0.775 but when hidden is credited, gap drops to 0.24, so weighted component = 0.2 × 40 = 8). Tenure saturated. No pledge signed, so no breach penalty. 4 red flags × 5 = 20. DO NOT PUBLISH — tiers only per PLAN.md.
